mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and risk premium, driving capital into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) during low FEDFUNDS regimes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5% indicates severe labor market slack that weakens wage income and consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: In walk-forward testing, S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with elevated unemployment signals a profit-margin expansion phase where output growth outpaces labor costs, generating positive S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE achieves a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during falsification.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, a sustained federal funds rate below 0.25% suppresses short-term liquidity premiums and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 one-week forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the 5-day forward return of S&P 500 is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerating above a 2% three-month threshold reflects robust consumer demand that translates into revenue surprises for S&P 500 firms, driving positive monthly forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of RSAFS acceleration produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with a low federal funds rate amplify monetary accommodation transmission into construction multipliers and real activity, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of HOUST and FEDFUNDS yields a 60-day Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls reflects aggregate income growth and consumption visibility, causing positive S&P 500 quarterly forward returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS momentum are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production over a three-month span signals contracting real output and corporate revenue pressure, producing negative S&P 500 monthly forward returns as margin expectations fall.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward falsification.
